title: Fragrant, Flaky Korean Vegetables Jorim
description: A traditional Korean dish featuring a variety of vegetables, perfect for a hangover cure or a comforting meal. This recipe comes from the Gangwon province of Korea, where it's often served as a side dish.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together sesame oil, vegetable oil, Korean chili flakes, and black pepper.
Add the chopped onion and minced garlic to the bowl and mix well.
Add the sliced mushrooms, zucchini, and bell pepper to the bowl and toss to coat.
Transfer the vegetables to a 9x13-inch baking dish and set aside.
In a small bowl, whisk together Korean chili paste, soy sauce, and sugar.
Pour the chili paste mixture over the vegetables in the baking dish.
Bake the vegetables in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes, or until they are tender and lightly caramelized.
Remove the vegetables from the oven and sprinkle with chopped green onions.
Serve the vegetables hot, garnished with additional green onions if desired.
Optional: Serve with steamed rice or noodles to soak up the savory sauce.
